finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_10.md:159; chapters/chapter_10.md:169; chapters/chapter_10.md:175; chapters/chapter_10.md:183; chapters/chapter_10.md:189; chapters/chapter_10.md:197
finding_results.notes: Reworked six of Derry's aftermath lines to restore his style-bible syntax. Line 159: and-chain plus restart ('because — because somebody') replaces clean declarative. Line 169: restart ('I was — I fed you the cues') and and-chain with repetition ('I was right there... I was right there') replace deposition-style subject-verb list. Line 175: 'But' opener, run-on chain, and trailing consequence clause ('because we said it was gone') replace fragmented declaratives. Line 183: and-chains ('and she'd been... and I just kept') plus restart ('I just kept — I kept') give the Lenna cake beat Derry's processing-aloud syntax; domestic detail still arrives at accusation indirectly before 'People were living in it.' Line 189: added trailing 'though' and em-dash run-on. Line 197: restart ('I'm not — I can't') at the head of Derry's refusal. Preserved continuity: Pell's name stays out of post-Caul dialogue per Ch11 boundary constraint. Core turn intact: Derry still connects fraud to his family's world, refuses to clean it up, and walks ahead.

summary: Single finding addressed. Derry's post-Caul accusation exchange was revised across six lines to replace clean moral-indictment declaratives with his established speech habits: and/but chains, visible restarts, trailing qualifications, and repetition. Four of six beats now carry visible Derry syntax (acceptance test required two). The Lenna cake beat at line 183 reaches the accusation indirectly through domestic detail before naming the fraud's consequence (acceptance test required one). Pell's name kept out of post-Caul dialogue to preserve Ch11 boundary continuity. No structural, pacing, or scene-architecture changes needed; all edits were local dialogue-register fixes.
